title: Stelis cobanensis
intro: Stelis cobanensis is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as support the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Stelis cobanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis cobanensis is often prepared as a tincture or infused tea, which is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Stelis cobanensis is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. The genus Stelis is classified within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae of the orchid family, Orchidaceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. Stelis cobanensis is native to Central America and belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The species is further classified within the family Orchidaceae, order Asparagales, and class Liliopsida.
